description_long: This sculpture depicts three lively, intertwined figures, likely children or cherubs, holding hands and dancing in a circle atop a round, textured base. The figures are rendered in a dynamic, expressive style, with detailed musculature and flowing hair, suggesting movement and joy. The base is roughly circular and appears to be made of the same material as the figures, with a weathered, stone-like texture. The figures' limbs are outstretched, and their bodies are slightly bent, enhancing the sense of motion and interaction. The surface of the sculpture has a dark, patinated finish, with hints of green and blue oxidation, typical of aged bronze. The overall composition is harmonious and balanced, inviting viewers to walk around and appreciate the scene from multiple angles.
description: A dynamic bronze sculpture of three figures dancing in a circle atop a round, textured base, with expressive details and a weathered patina.
description_view_0: From above, the circular arrangement of the three figures holding hands is clearly visible, emphasizing their interconnected movement.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the front-facing figure and the expressive gestures of the group, with the round base and textured surface visible.
description_view_2: A side view showing the dynamic posture of the figures, with one figure leaning back and the others supporting the circle.
description_view_3: This perspective accentuates the outstretched arms and the flowing lines of the figures, as well as the sculptural details of the base.
attribute_subject_matter: three dancing human figures in a circle
attribute_location_context: The sculpture appears to be designed for outdoor installation, likely in a park, plaza, or garden setting.
